title: Exchange rates
paperName: Cambridge International AS Level Economics (P1 & P2)
summary: 9708 AS exchange rates — floating and fixed systems, appreciation, depreciation, and effects on trade with GeoGebra.
learningObjectives: Define exchange rate and distinguish floating from fixed systems.
Explain causes of currency appreciation and depreciation.
Analyse effects on exports, imports, and AD.
Describe central bank intervention in forex markets.

simpleExplanation.title: Price of a currency
simpleExplanation.summary: Under floating rates, supply and demand for a currency set its price. Depreciation makes exports cheaper abroad and imports dearer at home.
steps: Appreciation: currency buys more foreign currency — ↑ import spending power.
Depreciation: boosts X competitiveness, raises M prices in domestic terms.
Demand for £ from foreigners buying UK goods/investments.
Fixed rate: central bank buys/sells reserves to maintain peg.

sections.content: **Exchange rates** (6.4) feature in **J-curve** and **Marshall–Lerner** discussions at A Level. At AS, focus on **AD transmission** via (X−M).
